name: Scrub Daddy
tagline: The smiley-face sponge that changes texture with water temperature
description: Scrub Daddy is a smiley-face shaped sponge that becomes firm in cold water for tough scrubbing and soft in warm water for gentle cleaning. It's odor-resistant, scratch-free on delicate surfaces, and one of the best-selling products in Shark Tank history.
sharkUpdate: Lori Greiner remains an active partner and brand ambassador. Scrub Daddy is one of her most celebrated investments.

timeline.event: Founder Aaron Krause invents the scrubber for his car detailing business
Appears on Shark Tank Season 4. Lori Greiner invests $200K for 20%
Appears on QVC — sells out in 7 minutes. $18M in sales in first year
Expands to major retailers: Walmart, Target, Bed Bath & Beyond
Lifetime sales surpass $900M. Launches Scrub Mommy, Scrub Daisy and more

currentStatus: Still thriving — over $900M in lifetime sales. Biggest Shark Tank success story.

name: Bombas
tagline: The most comfortable socks ever made — buy one, donate one
description: Bombas makes premium athletic socks engineered for comfort with a buy-one-donate-one model. They've donated over 100 million items to homeless shelters.
sharkUpdate: Daymond John remains an investor and mentor. Bombas has grown far beyond his initial involvement.

timeline.event: Founders Randy Goldberg and David Heath discover socks are #1 requested item at homeless shelters
Launch Indiegogo campaign, raise $145K in 30 days
Appear on Shark Tank Season 6. Daymond John invests $200K
Donate 1 millionth pair of socks
Revenue exceeds $300M annually. Expand into t-shirts and underwear
100 million items donated to those in need

currentStatus: One of Shark Tank's greatest success stories — $300M+ annual revenue, 100M+ items donated.

name: Ring (Doorbot)
tagline: Video doorbell that lets you see and speak to visitors from anywhere
description: Ring started as 'Doorbot' — a Wi-Fi enabled video doorbell. Despite all sharks declining, Ring grew to dominate the smart home security market.
sharkUpdate: Jamie Siminoff returned to Shark Tank in Season 10 as a guest shark — the ultimate revenge arc.

timeline.event: Jamie Siminoff invents a Wi-Fi doorbell in his garage
Pitches as 'Doorbot' on Shark Tank. All 5 sharks decline
Rebrands to Ring. Raises $28M in funding
Launches Ring Alarm home security system
Amazon acquires Ring for $1.2 billion
Siminoff appears on Shark Tank Season 10 as a guest shark

currentStatus: Acquired by Amazon for $1.2B — the most famous 'no deal' in Shark Tank history.

name: Squatty Potty
tagline: The toilet stool that changes the way you go
description: Squatty Potty is a bathroom stool designed to elevate your feet while sitting on the toilet, mimicking a natural squatting position. Famous for its viral unicorn commercial.
sharkUpdate: Lori Greiner remains an investor. She helped produce the famous viral unicorn ad that became one of YouTube's most-watched commercials.

timeline.event: Judy Edwards creates the Squatty Potty to help with her own digestive issues
Appears on Shark Tank Season 6. Lori Greiner invests $350K for 10%
Viral unicorn commercial racks up 100M+ views. Sales explode to $33M
Available in Target, Bed Bath & Beyond, and Costco nationwide
Lifetime sales exceed $200M

currentStatus: Went viral after Shark Tank — $33M in sales within a year of the episode.

name: Cousins Maine Lobster
tagline: Fresh Maine lobster rolls served from food trucks across America
description: Two cousins from Maine started a food truck serving authentic Maine lobster rolls in LA. Barbara Corcoran helped them franchise into a nationwide food truck empire.
sharkUpdate: Barbara Corcoran helped develop their franchise model and remains a proud investor.

timeline.event: Jim Tselikis and Sabin Lomac launch a single food truck in Los Angeles
Appear on Shark Tank Season 4. Barbara Corcoran invests $55K for 15%
Expand to multiple trucks and begin franchising
30+ franchise locations operating across the US
Over $50M in lifetime revenue. Brick-and-mortar locations added

currentStatus: Expanded to 30+ franchise locations across the US.

name: Tipsy Elves
tagline: Ugly Christmas sweaters and festive apparel for adults who love to party
description: Tipsy Elves creates intentionally ugly Christmas sweaters and fun holiday apparel. Robert Herjavec invested and helped them scale into a multi-million dollar seasonal brand.
sharkUpdate: Robert Herjavec remains an investor. He called it one of his most fun investments.

timeline.event: Two lawyers Evan Mendelsohn and Nick Morton start selling ugly sweaters online
Appear on Shark Tank Season 5. Robert Herjavec invests $100K for 10%
Expand beyond Christmas into Halloween, St. Patrick's Day, and summer apparel
Over $70M in cumulative sales
Lifetime sales exceed $125M. Available in major retailers

currentStatus: Over $125M in lifetime sales.

name: Kodiak Cakes
tagline: High-protein pancake and waffle mix made with 100% whole grains
description: Kodiak Cakes makes protein-packed pancake and waffle mixes from whole grains. Despite not getting a deal on Shark Tank, they became one of the top pancake mix brands in America.
sharkUpdate: No shark invested, but Kevin O'Leary later admitted it was one of his biggest mistakes.

timeline.event: Joel Clark's mother creates the original whole grain pancake recipe
Joel Clark starts selling the mix door-to-door from a red wagon as a kid
Appears on Shark Tank Season 5. All sharks decline
Lands on Target shelves. Revenue triples year-over-year
Revenue exceeds $200M. L Catterton acquires majority stake
Files for IPO. Valued at over $1 billion

currentStatus: No deal taken — grew to $200M+ revenue and filed for IPO. Valued at $1B+.

name: Simply Fit Board
tagline: The workout board with a twist — balance and core training made fun
description: Simply Fit Board is a balance board that works your core while doing simple twisting motions. Lori Greiner invested and it became one of QVC's best-selling fitness products.
sharkUpdate: Lori Greiner helped launch it on QVC where it generated over $9M in a single day.

timeline.event: Linda Clark and Gloria Hoffman invent the balance board
Appear on Shark Tank Season 7. Lori Greiner invests $125K for 20%
Launches on QVC — sells $9M worth in a single day
Cumulative sales exceed $100M
Total lifetime sales reach $160M+

currentStatus: Over $160M in sales — one of the highest-grossing Shark Tank products ever.

name: The Original Comfy
tagline: A giant wearable blanket hoodie that went viral overnight
description: The Original Comfy is an oversized blanket with sleeves and a hood. It went viral after appearing on Shark Tank and sold out within hours.
sharkUpdate: Barbara Corcoran called it one of her best investments. The brothers went on to star in their own reality show.

timeline.event: Brothers Brian and Michael Speciale create the wearable blanket
Appear on Shark Tank Season 9. Barbara Corcoran invests $50K for 30%
Sells out on Amazon the night the episode airs. Goes viral on social media
Cumulative sales hit $25M
Lifetime sales exceed $150M. Brothers star in CNBC reality show

currentStatus: Over $150M in sales — one of the most viral Shark Tank products ever.

name: GrooveBook
tagline: Monthly photo book subscription — 100 photos printed and mailed for $2.99
description: GrooveBook was a subscription service that printed 100 smartphone photos into a perforated photo book monthly for $2.99. Acquired by Shutterfly for $14.5M.
sharkUpdate: Mark Cuban and Kevin O'Leary both received a return on their investment when Shutterfly acquired the company.

timeline.event: Brian and Julie Whiteman launch GrooveBook app
Appear on Shark Tank Season 5. Cuban and O'Leary invest $150K for 80%
App downloads surge after episode. Subscription base hits 500K+
Acquired by Shutterfly for $14.5 million just one year after Shark Tank

currentStatus: Acquired by Shutterfly for $14.5M — a huge exit for a $150K investment.

name: Dude Wipes
tagline: Flushable wipes made specifically for men
description: Dude Wipes are flushable personal hygiene wipes marketed to men with bold branding. Mark Cuban invested and helped them grow into a major retail brand.
sharkUpdate: Mark Cuban remains an active investor. The founders credit him with helping secure major retail distribution.

timeline.event: Four college friends create Dude Wipes in a Chicago apartment
Appear on Shark Tank Season 6. Mark Cuban invests $75K for 25%
Launch in Target stores nationwide
Available in Walmart, CVS, Walgreens, and major retailers
Revenue exceeds $100M. Become dominant brand in the category

currentStatus: Over $100M in revenue — now in Walmart, Target, CVS, and major retailers nationwide.

name: Lovepop
tagline: Intricate 3D pop-up greeting cards engineered with ship-building techniques
description: Lovepop creates stunning 3D pop-up greeting cards using a paper engineering technique inspired by ship architecture. Kevin O'Leary invested and helped them scale massively.
sharkUpdate: Kevin O'Leary remains an investor and helped Lovepop scale through retail partnerships.

timeline.event: Two MIT naval engineers John Wise and Wombi Rose found Lovepop
Appear on Shark Tank Season 7. Kevin O'Leary invests $300K for 15%
Land in Hallmark stores, Barnes & Noble, and airports nationwide
Revenue exceeds $20M. Pop-up shops in major airports
Expand internationally. Cards sold in 40+ countries

currentStatus: Over $20M in revenue — now a leading greeting card brand in major retailers.

name: The Bouqs Co.
tagline: Farm-fresh flowers delivered directly from eco-friendly farms
description: The Bouqs Co. sources flowers directly from sustainable volcanic farms. Kevin O'Leary famously walked out during the pitch, but they later raised $24M.
sharkUpdate: Kevin O'Leary famously walked out mid-pitch. Founder John Tabis later said the exposure alone was worth millions.

timeline.event: John Tabis and Juan Pablo Montufar co-found The Bouqs Co.
Appear on Shark Tank Season 5. Kevin O'Leary walks out. No deal made
Raise $24M in Series B funding — sharks missed out
Partnership with 1-800-Flowers falls through but brand grows independently
Acquired by FTD for $100M+

currentStatus: Acquired by FTD for $100M+ after raising $24M post-show. Classic 'shark mistake.'

name: Breathometer
tagline: The world's first smartphone breathalyzer
description: Breathometer was a pocket-sized breathalyzer that plugged into your smartphone. It received investment from all five sharks — one of the rarest events in Shark Tank history.
sharkUpdate: All 5 sharks invested but the FTC later ordered refunds to customers after accuracy issues. A cautionary tale.

timeline.event: Charles Michael Yim creates the smartphone breathalyzer
Appears on Shark Tank Season 5. All 5 sharks invest $1M — historic moment
Sales surge. Launches Mint breath analyzer product
FTC investigation into accuracy claims
FTC orders refunds to customers. Company effectively shuts down

currentStatus: All 5 sharks invested — but the FTC later ordered customer refunds over accuracy issues.

name: Sand Cloud
tagline: Turkish towels and beach accessories that help save marine life
description: Sand Cloud sells premium Turkish towels and beach accessories, donating a portion of profits to marine conservation. Barbara Corcoran invested.
sharkUpdate: Barbara Corcoran remains an investor and has praised their social media growth strategy.

timeline.event: Three San Diego friends start selling Turkish towels out of a van
Appear on Shark Tank Season 8. Barbara Corcoran invests $200K for 15%
Reach $5M in revenue. Strong Instagram following drives sales
Over $10M in revenue. Expand product line to apparel
Donate over $1M to marine conservation organizations

currentStatus: Multi-million dollar brand with a strong social following and marine conservation mission.

name: Scholly
tagline: App that matches students with college scholarships they qualify for
description: Scholly is an app that helps students find college scholarships. The pitch caused a rare argument between sharks, with Lori and Daymond investing against the others' objections.
sharkUpdate: Lori Greiner and Daymond John remain investors. Their heated argument with other sharks became one of the most replayed Shark Tank moments.

timeline.event: Christopher Gray creates Scholly to help students find scholarships
Appears on Shark Tank Season 6. Causes famous shark argument. Lori and Daymond invest
App hits #1 on App Store. 500K+ users
Helped students find over $100M in scholarships
Over 5 million app downloads. Expands to career services

currentStatus: Helped students find $100M+ in scholarships. One of the most downloaded education apps.

name: Bottle Breacher
tagline: Bottle openers made from decommissioned .50 caliber bullets by veterans
description: Bottle Breacher makes handcrafted bottle openers from decommissioned .50 caliber bullets, made by military veterans.
sharkUpdate: Mark Cuban and Kevin O'Leary both remain investors. The company is beloved in the military community.

timeline.event: Navy SEAL Eli Crane starts making bottle openers from spent .50 cal rounds
Appears on Shark Tank Season 6. Cuban and O'Leary invest $150K for 20%
Sales exceed $1M. Ships internationally
Eli Crane runs for Arizona Congress
Crane wins Arizona Congressional seat. Lifetime sales over $12M

currentStatus: Over $12M in sales. Founder Eli Crane went on to win a seat in US Congress.

name: Grace & Lace
tagline: Lace boot socks and leg warmers that are fashionable and cozy
description: Grace & Lace creates decorative lace boot socks and leg warmers. Barbara Corcoran invested after one of the most emotional pitches in Shark Tank history.
sharkUpdate: Barbara Corcoran remains an investor and has called Melissa Hinnant one of her most inspiring entrepreneurs.

timeline.event: Melissa and Rick Hinnant found Grace & Lace after losing a premature baby
Business grows to $1.6M in first year with zero ad spend
Appear on Shark Tank Season 5. Barbara Corcoran invests $175K for 10%
Annual revenue hits $5M. Expand to full women's apparel line
Revenue exceeds $10M annually

currentStatus: Still operating with multi-million dollar annual revenue.

name: Drop Stop
tagline: Fills the gap between your car seat and console so things stop falling in
description: Drop Stop is a wedge-shaped car accessory that fills the gap between your seat and center console. Lori Greiner invested and the product became a QVC sensation.
sharkUpdate: Lori Greiner remains an investor and helped them launch on QVC.

timeline.event: Marc Newburger and Jeffrey Simon invent Drop Stop
Appear on Shark Tank Season 4. Lori Greiner invests $300K for 20%
Launched on QVC. Sells out multiple times
Cumulative sales exceed $10M
Lifetime sales over $15M. Amazon bestseller

currentStatus: Over $15M in sales, still a bestseller on Amazon and QVC.

name: Simple Sugars
tagline: All-natural sugar scrubs safe for sensitive skin, created by a teenager
description: Simple Sugars creates all-natural sugar scrubs designed for sensitive skin. Founded by Lani Lazzari at age 11, she pitched at 18 and received investment from Mark Cuban.
sharkUpdate: Mark Cuban remains an investor and has called Lani one of the most impressive entrepreneurs he's met on the show.

timeline.event: 11-year-old Lani Lazzari creates the scrub to help with her own eczema
Starts selling at local craft fairs and farmers markets
Appears on Shark Tank Season 4 at age 18. Mark Cuban invests $100K for 33%
Sales go from $30K to $1M+ in first year post-show
Revenue exceeds $10M annually. Sold in major retailers

currentStatus: Sales went from $30K to $1M+ in first year. Now a multi-million dollar skincare brand.

name: ReadeREST
tagline: Magnetic eyeglass holder that clips to your shirt without scratching lenses
description: ReadeREST is a simple magnetic clip that holds reading glasses securely to your shirt without damaging lenses. Lori Greiner invested and turned it into a QVC mega-hit.
sharkUpdate: Lori Greiner took a majority stake and helped grow it into one of QVC's top-selling products.

timeline.event: Rick Hopper invents the magnetic eyeglass holder
Appears on Shark Tank Season 3. Lori Greiner invests $150K for 65%
Launches on QVC. Sells $1M in first appearance
Cumulative sales exceed $10M
Lifetime sales over $20M. Sold in 5,000+ retail locations

currentStatus: Over $20M in sales — one of Lori's most beloved QVC products.

name: Wicked Good Cupcakes
tagline: Gourmet cupcakes in a jar, shipped fresh anywhere in the US
description: Wicked Good Cupcakes sells gourmet cupcakes layered in mason jars. Kevin O'Leary invested in a royalty deal rather than equity.
sharkUpdate: Kevin O'Leary took a royalty deal instead of equity — $1 per jar sold until he recouped $3x his investment.

timeline.event: Tracy and Dani Vilagie (mother and daughter) start baking cupcakes in jars
Launch online store. Grow via word of mouth
Appear on Shark Tank Season 4. Kevin O'Leary invests $75K royalty deal
Revenue triples post-show. Ship 50K jars per month
Multi-million dollar business shipping nationwide

currentStatus: Multi-million dollar business shipping nationwide. Still family-owned.

name: Sleep Styler
tagline: Heat-free hair rollers you wear to bed for curls while you sleep
description: Sleep Styler are absorbent, heat-free hair rollers designed to be worn overnight, giving salon-quality curls without heat damage.
sharkUpdate: Lori Greiner helped launch it on Amazon where it became an instant bestseller.

timeline.event: Tara Brown creates the heat-free roller concept
Runs successful Kickstarter campaign
Appears on Shark Tank Season 8. Lori Greiner invests $75K for 25%
Sells over $1M on Amazon in the first month post-episode
Available in Target, Ulta, and major retailers

currentStatus: Sold over $1M on Amazon in first month post-show. Available in major beauty retailers.

name: Bala Bangles
tagline: Stylish weighted wristbands and anklebands for low-impact workouts
description: Bala Bangles are sleek, fashionable weighted bands worn on wrists or ankles. Maria Sharapova and Mark Cuban both invested.
sharkUpdate: Mark Cuban and Maria Sharapova remain investors. Bala is now a staple in fitness boutiques and celebrity home gyms.

timeline.event: Max Kislevitz and Natalie Holloway found Bala in Los Angeles
Gain celebrity following. Featured in major fitness publications
Appear on Shark Tank Season 11. Mark Cuban and Maria Sharapova invest $900K
COVID fitness boom drives massive sales increase
Available in Equinox, Soul Cycle, and 1,000+ retail locations. $30M+ in sales

currentStatus: Available in Equinox, Soul Cycle, and 1,000+ locations. $30M+ in sales.

name: Manscaped
tagline: Below-the-waist grooming tools designed specifically for men
description: Manscaped makes grooming products specifically designed for men's body grooming. Despite no deal, grew to $100M+ revenue independently.
sharkUpdate: All sharks passed. Manscaped went on to become one of the biggest 'ones that got away' in recent Shark Tank history.

timeline.event: Paul Tran founds Manscaped in San Diego
Launch viral advertising campaign
Appear on Shark Tank Season 11. All sharks decline
Revenue explodes to $100M+ through direct-to-consumer model
Go public via SPAC deal. Valued at $1 billion

currentStatus: No deal — grew to $100M+ revenue, went public via SPAC, valued at $1B.

name: Coffee Meets Bagel
tagline: Dating app that sends you one quality match per day
description: Coffee Meets Bagel is a dating app that curates one match per day for quality over quantity. The founders famously turned down Mark Cuban's $30M buyout offer.
sharkUpdate: Mark Cuban offered to buy the whole company for $30M on the spot. The Kang sisters said no. Memorable TV.

timeline.event: Sisters Arum, Dawoon, and Soo Kang launch Coffee Meets Bagel
Appear on Shark Tank Season 6. Mark Cuban offers $30M for 100% — they decline
Raise $7.8M in Series A funding
Over 50M matches made. App used in 40+ countries
Data breach affects 6M users but company survives and grows

currentStatus: Turned down $30M from Mark Cuban — still operating as one of the top dating apps.

name: The Lip Bar
tagline: Bold, vegan lipsticks in shades for all skin tones
description: The Lip Bar is a vegan, cruelty-free lipstick brand. Despite being rejected by all sharks, the founder grew it into a brand now sold in Target.
sharkUpdate: Kevin O'Leary called it a 'disaster.' Founder Melissa Butler went on to prove him wrong spectacularly.

timeline.event: Melissa Butler quits her Wall Street job to start The Lip Bar
Appears on Shark Tank Season 6. All sharks reject. Kevin O'Leary calls it a disaster
Lands in Target stores — the same place O'Leary said she'd never be
Available in 1,500+ Target stores. Featured on CNN, Forbes, Essence
Revenue exceeds $10M annually. Expands to skincare

currentStatus: Rejected by all sharks — now sold in 1,500+ Target stores. One of the best comeback stories.

name: Mission Belt
tagline: Ratchet belts without holes — a perfect fit every time
description: Mission Belt makes leather belts with a ratchet buckle system instead of holes, allowing a precise fit in quarter-inch increments.
sharkUpdate: Daymond John took an unusual loan structure rather than equity. The company has grown significantly since.

timeline.event: Nate Holzapfel and Steve Finnell found Mission Belt
Appear on Shark Tank Season 4. Daymond John invests in unusual loan deal
Revenue exceeds $1M. Available in major menswear retailers
Launch women's line and accessories
Multi-million dollar brand available nationwide

currentStatus: Multi-million dollar belt brand available nationwide in major retailers.

name: Tower Paddle Boards
tagline: Direct-to-consumer paddle boards at half the retail price
description: Tower Paddle Boards sells inflatable stand-up paddle boards directly to consumers at affordable prices. Mark Cuban invested.
sharkUpdate: Mark Cuban has called Tower one of his best early Shark Tank investments.

timeline.event: Stephan Aarstol founds Tower Paddle Boards in San Diego
Appears on Shark Tank Season 3. Mark Cuban invests $150K for 30%
Named to Inc. 500 fastest growing companies
Experiments with 5-hour workday — featured in major media
Lifetime sales exceed $30M. Expanded to full beach lifestyle brand

currentStatus: Over $30M in lifetime sales — one of Mark Cuban's best early investments.
